content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 1 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 99v–100v | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_001_page_001.jpg, DEU_001_page_002.jpg, DEU_001_page_003.jpg; source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/DEU_001_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_001_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** These are the words Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan — in the desert, in the Arabah, over against Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab. **2** It is eleven days' journey from Horeb by way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.

**3** In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses told the sons of Israel everything the LORD had commanded him to say to them. **4** This was after he had defeated Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and Og king of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth, at Edrei.

**5** Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to make plain this instruction, saying:

**6** "The LORD our God spoke to us at Horeb: 'You have stayed long enough at this mountain. **7** Break camp and set out for the hill country of the Amorites and all their neighbors — the Arabah, the highlands, the Shephelah, the Negev, the seacoast — the land of the Canaanites, and Lebanon, all the way to the great river, the Euphrates. **8** Look — I have placed this land before you. Go in and take possession of the land the LORD swore to your fathers — Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — to give them and their descendants after them.'

**9** "At that time I told you: 'I cannot carry you on my own. **10** The LORD your God has multiplied you until today you are as numerous as the stars of the sky. **11** May the LORD, the God of your fathers, multiply you a thousand times over and bless you just as he promised! **12** But how can I bear alone the burden of you and your disputes and your quarrels? **13** Pick out wise, discerning, and experienced men from your tribes, and I will appoint them as your leaders.' **14** You answered me: 'What you have said is good.'

**15** "So I took the leaders of your tribes — wise and experienced men — and appointed them as leaders over you: commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officials for your tribes. **16** And I charged your judges at that time: 'Hear the cases between your fellow Israelites, and judge fairly between a person and a fellow Israelite, and between the native and the sojourner. **17** You must not show partiality in judgment. Listen to the small and the great alike. Do not be intimidated by anyone, for judgment belongs to God. Bring to me any case that is too hard for you, and I will hear it.' **18** And at that time I told you everything you were to do.

**19** "Then we set out from Horeb and marched through that whole terrifying wilderness — the great and fearful desert you have seen — on our way to the hill country of the Amorites, as the LORD our God commanded us, until we came to Kadesh-barnea. **20** There I said to you: 'You have reached the hill country of the Amorites that the LORD our God is giving us. **21** Look — the LORD your God has placed this land before you. Go up and take possession of it, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has told you. Do not be afraid. Do not lose heart.'

**22** "Then all of you came to me and said: 'Let us send men ahead to scout the land for us and bring back word about what route we should take and what towns we will find.' **23** The plan seemed good to me, so I selected twelve of you, one from each tribe. **24** They set out into the hill country, came to the Valley of Eshcol, and explored it. **25** They picked some of the fruit of the land and brought it back to us. They gave us this report: 'It is a good land that the LORD our God is giving us.'

**26** "But you refused to go up. You rebelled against the command of the LORD your God. **27** You grumbled in your tents and said: 'Because the LORD hates us he brought us out of Egypt to hand us over to the Amorites and destroy us. **28** Where can we go? Our brothers have discouraged us, saying the people there are bigger and taller than we are; their cities are large and walled to the sky. And we even saw the Anakim there!'

**29** "I said to you: 'Do not be terrified of them. Do not be afraid. **30** The LORD your God, who goes before you, will fight for you himself — just as he did everything for you in Egypt before your eyes, **31** and in the wilderness, where you saw how the LORD your God carried you, as a father carries his child, all the way until you reached this place.' **32** Yet even with all this, you did not trust the LORD your God, **33** who went ahead of you on the journey — in fire by night to show you the way, and in cloud by day — to seek out the places where you would camp.

**34** "The LORD heard the sound of your words and was furious. He swore: **35** 'Not one of these — not a single person from this evil generation — shall see the good land I swore to give your fathers, **36** except Caleb son of Jephunneh. He shall see it. I will give him and his descendants the land he walked on, because he followed the LORD wholeheartedly.'

**37** "The LORD was even angry with me on your account, saying: 'You too will not enter there. **38** Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you — he will enter. Strengthen him, because he is the one who will lead Israel to inherit it. **39** And your little ones, who you said would become plunder — your children who today do not yet know good from evil — they will enter the land. I will give it to them, and they will possess it. **40** But as for you — turn around and march back into the wilderness, on the road to the Red Sea.'

**41** "Then you answered me: 'We have sinned against the LORD. We will go up and fight, just as the LORD our God commanded us.' And each of you buckled on his weapons, thinking it would be easy to go up into the hill country. **42** But the LORD said to me: 'Tell them, Do not go up and do not fight — for I am not among you. You will be struck down before your enemies.' **43** I told you this, but you would not listen. You defied the LORD's command and arrogantly marched up into the hill country. **44** The Amorites living there came out against you and chased you like a swarm of bees. They cut you down from Seir all the way to Hormah. **45** You came back and wept before the LORD, but the LORD paid no attention to your weeping and gave you no hearing. **46** So you stayed at Kadesh many days — the long days you remained there."

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Dynamic choices — PNEUMA:**

**V.2 — "eleven days' journey":** The editorial aside is biting irony: what should have been a short journey became forty years of wandering because of disobedience. PNEUMA lets this land with full impact by placing it immediately after the geographic list.

**V.5 — "undertook to make plain this instruction":** Hebrew be'er et-ha-torah — literally "to make clear/dig out the law." The image is of excavating and clarifying, not merely reciting. PNEUMA renders "make plain" to capture the pedagogical, expository nature of Deuteronomy as a whole.

**V.12 — "burden of you and your disputes and your quarrels":** Three nouns in Hebrew (torach, massa, rivkhem) — the weight of leading a contentious people. PNEUMA renders all three to preserve Moses' tone of exasperation.

**V.17 — "Do not be intimidated by anyone":** Hebrew lo taguru mipney ish — "do not shrink/be afraid before the face of man." PNEUMA's "intimidated" carries both the fear and the political pressure implied in the judicial context.

**V.27 — "Because the LORD hates us":** The accusation is shocking — Israel inverts the covenant relationship. PNEUMA does not soften this. The raw irony: God who loved them enough to redeem them is accused of hatred.

**V.31 — "as a father carries his child":** This simile is one of the most tender images in Deuteronomy. PNEUMA preserves it with full warmth — the LORD is no distant deity but a parent doing the heavy lifting.

**V.44 — "chased you like a swarm of bees":** The simile (ka-asher ta'asenah ha-devorim) is vivid and humiliating — Israel, the people of the great redemption, flees stinging insects. PNEUMA keeps it unvarnished.

**Vaticanus (LXX) observation:** The LXX Vaticanus page 143–144 reads smoothly through this chapter. Notably in v.1 the LXX renders "be-ever ha-yarden" (beyond the Jordan) as "peran tou Iordanou," a straightforward rendering. The LXX at v.17 adds "hoti he krisis tou theou estin" — identical to the Hebrew "ki ha-mishpat l'Elohim hu." The LXX consistently uses "kyrios" for YHWH throughout, confirming the Vaticanus tradition's standard practice. The folio numbering on Vaticanus page 143 is clearly visible.
